claims.claim: open-slide renders every page into a fixed 1920 × 1080 React canvas (1920x1080)
claims.raw_snippet: a slide framework built for agents; fixed 1920 × 1080 canvas (1920x1080); CANVAS_WIDTH / CANVAS_HEIGHT exports

claims.claim: open-slide projects are scaffolded with `npx @open-slide/cli init <name>`
Page, SlideMeta, and DesignSystem are types exported from @open-slide/core; ImagePlaceholder is a value export
claims.raw_snippet: @open-slide/core exports types Page, SlideMeta, DesignSystem and value ImagePlaceholder

claims.claim: Steps and Step are progressive-reveal components exported from @open-slide/core (added open-slide core 1.10.0)
claims.raw_snippet: Stepped reveals (`<Steps>` / `<Step>`) ... import { Step, Steps } from '@open-slide/core'; <Step> fades in over duration ms (default 180)

claims.claim: open-slide ships five built-in skills (slide-authoring, create-slide, create-theme, apply-comments, current-slide); the /create-slide picker lists available themes
claims.raw_snippet: packages/core/skills contains exactly: slide-authoring, create-slide, create-theme, apply-comments, current-slide; running /create-slide lists themes as picker options

claims.claim: The inspector drops {/* @slide-comment ... */} markers in source for the apply-comments workflow
The live slide cursor is written to node_modules/.open-slide/current.json
claims.raw_snippet: node_modules/.open-slide/current.json, slideId, pageIndex, pageNumber, totalPages, slideTitle, selection

claims.claim: The skill's Reference Links and font-loading examples cite these external sources: open-slide docs, MDN logical-properties and bdi pages, the Google Fonts Hebrew subset, the upstream create-slide and create-theme skills
Hebrew renders ~10-15% wider per character than Latin at the same px size, so the skill bumps the Hebrew type scale: hero +10-15%, section heading +10%, page heading +8%
claims.raw_snippet: Hebrew type-scale calibration: hero +10-15%, section +10%, page heading +8%; Hebrew is ~10-15% wider per character at the same font-size

claims.claim: open-slide page roots fill the canvas with width and height 100%; the brand-discipline example uses a solid 0% label rather than a gradient
claims.raw_snippet: The root element of each page should fill the canvas: width 100%, height 100%. Brand example: a confident solid 0% beats a contrived gradient.

claims.claim: Hebrew currency notation uses ₪1,500 (symbol-first) or 1,500 ש״ח (suffix); NIS 1,500 reads as foreign to a Hebrew audience
claims.raw_snippet: Israeli Shekel notation example: ₪1,500 or 1,500 ש״ח; do not write NIS 1,500 for a Hebrew audience. The ₪ symbol (U+20AA) renders in all listed Google Fonts.
